content: A photorealistic top-down product-style photograph of a single luxury tasting menu printed on bright white textured paper and placed on a dark walnut wooden tabletop, with soft warm ambient lighting and gentle natural shadows. The sheet is portrait orientation, slightly rotated clockwise, occupying most of the frame with clean margins around it. The design is an ultra-minimal fine-dining menu with elegant editorial typography, lots of negative space, precise alignment, and a refined black-and-dark-gray ink palette. At the upper left, the restaurant identity appears in 3 stacked lines: a large serif word {argument name="restaurant name" default="Silence"}, below it 2 Chinese characters "沈默", and below that a large serif French line "Le Silence". Under the title is a small location line reading "Ginza, Tokyo B3F". At the upper right is a small date line in French followed by Japanese date text: {argument name="date text" default="22 Avril 2026 • 令和八年四月二十二日"}. The main body is organized into 3 vertical content zones: a left numbered course list, a middle translated course list, and a far-right icon column. The left list contains exactly 9 numbered courses labeled 1 through 9, each with French title and Japanese subtitle and an English ingredient line beneath; the 9 visible course titles are: "Huître de Bretagne, eau de mer", "Daurade de Kyoto, pétale de sakura", "Oursin d’Hokkaidō, consommé d’algue", "Asperge de Nemuro, sauce mousseline", "Homard de Miyako, bisque légère", "Canard de Challans, figue et balsamique", "Boeuf de Kagoshima, truffe noire", "Fromage de chèvre, miel du Japon", and "Agrume, thé vert et gin". The middle list repeats the 9 dishes in a second compact column with French titles and Japanese descriptions. The far-right column contains exactly 9 horizontal icon rows aligned to the courses, each row showing small monochrome hand-drawn ingredient or allergen pictograms; some rows have 2 icons and some 3, for a total of 19 icons overall. Near the lower left is a section titled "Le mot du chef" followed by a short French paragraph in small serif text. Below that is a section titled "Origine des ingrédients / 食材の産地" with a dense 2-row ingredient-origin grid listing exactly 15 numbered origin entries in tiny text. In the lower right quadrant, include a small label "Chef" above a large sweeping handwritten signature reading {argument name="chef name" default="Keiichiro Aoyama"}. Along the very bottom edge is a thin horizontal rule and a centered allergen legend line with exactly 7 small outline icons and labels: shellfish, crustaceans, egg, fish, milk, gluten, sulfites, each labeled in French with Japanese beneath. The overall impression should be high-end Michelin-style menu design, editorial restraint, immaculate spacing, subtle paper grain, sharply legible text hierarchy, and realistic photography rather than a flat scan.
title: Luxury Bilingual Tasting Menu Photo
description: A realistic overhead photograph of an elegant French-Japanese fine-dining menu sheet, ideal for showcasing AI-generated editorial menu design systems.

content: Create a sophisticated multi-page brand visual manual for a company named "{argument name="brand name" default="Any Brand Name"}". The overall brand persona should be playful, futuristic, vibrant, and highly designed, striking a balance between "toy-like energy" and the "sophistication of a high-end creative tech brand." The visual style should use bold and experimental brand language, including: high-saturation bright colors, simple geometric shapes, modern typography, dynamic visual rhythm, and slightly surreal image expression. The overall atmosphere should convey: creativity, youthfulness, rhythm, tactile experience, and conceptual avant-garde. The expression needs to integrate the fun of consumer products, the presentation of fashion brands, and the language of contemporary digital design. The brand manual should reflect: extreme focus on product details, diverse visual experimentation, packaging design exploration, editorial style photography, immersive retail experience, and cross-platform brand application. The final presentation should construct a confident, contemporary, unified, and complete brand worldview—both easy to understand and approachable, yet possessing a strong sense of art direction, maintaining high visual consistency while showcasing aesthetic standards at a high-end creative studio level.

content: Create a clean, premium Japanese landing page hero image for a fictional tax accountant office specializing in inheritance tax consultation. The design should look like a modern listing-ad LP first view, wide desktop format, with a soft ivory and warm white background, muted sage green and gold accent colors, elegant serif Japanese typography for the headline, and a calm trustworthy atmosphere. On the left upper area, place a refined circular logo with a thin gold ring, a large serif letter C, and a small botanical line illustration, followed by the office name 税理士事務所 {argument name="office name" default="C"} and the English subtitle TAX ACCOUNTANT OFFICE {argument name="office name" default="C"}. content: Create a clean minimalist static ad on an off-white light gray studio background with a centered editorial layout. At the top, place a large luxury-fashion-style serif headline in black reading {argument name="headline text" default="Stop gambling with your ad spend."}, with the word "gambling" set in italic serif for emphasis. In the middle, show a vintage metal slot-machine window viewed straight on, slightly weathered and realistic, with 3 vertical reels inside a rectangular steel housing and a lever with a black knob on the right side. The 3 reels must display, from left to right, "$", "$", and "?". Make the rightmost reel background pale pink while the first 2 reels are aged white. Add a hand-drawn neon-pink annotation style over the image: 1 arrow-and-note on the left pointing to the first reel reading {argument name="left annotation" default="your attribution"}, 1 starburst scribble above the pink question-mark reel, 1 arrow from the question-mark reel to a circled note on the right reading {argument name="right annotation" default="actual revenue"}, and 1 large curved arrow in the lower left pointing toward the body copy area. Below the slot machine, center a short sans-serif paragraph in black reading {argument name="body text" default="See which campaigns drive revenue, not just clicks. Connect Meta, Google, and Stripe in 60 seconds."}. At the bottom center, place a simple black analytics-style bar logo icon followed by the bold sans-serif brand name {argument name="brand name" default="Humblytics"}. Use strong text fidelity, polished product-ad composition, soft studio shadows, restrained palette of black, steel gray, off-white, and hot pink, and a modern direct-response ad aesthetic.
title: Minimal Slot Machine Attribution Ad
description: This prompt generates a polished static marketing ad with a vintage slot machine metaphor, handwritten annotations, and strong centered typography for analytics or attribution software promotions.

content: Create a bold static SaaS ad on a matte black background with a premium editorial look and highly accurate typography. Center the composition around a large curled, crumpled receipt made of white thermal paper, angled slightly from upper left to lower right, with visible paper texture, soft shadows, and rolled ends on both the upper right and lower left. On the receipt, show exactly 5 line items in black monospaced or receipt-style text aligned in two columns: "GA4" with "$0", "Hotjar" with "$99", "Optimizely" with "$2,450", "GTM setup (dev hours)" with "$1,800", and "Tag manager debug" with "$400". Highlight the word "Optimizely" with a jagged magenta starburst sticker behind it. Add a dashed divider line below the items, then a bold "TOTAL" label on the lower left of the receipt. On the lower right of the receipt, place a large price reading "{argument name="monthly price" default="$19/mo"}" in heavy black type, circled with 2 hand-drawn magenta marker loops. Add 3 handwritten magenta annotation elements: on the left side, the words "{argument name="left annotation" default="one tool"}" in brush script with an underline and a curved arrow pointing to the receipt; near the bottom center, a curved magenta arrow pointing upward toward the circled price; and at the bottom right, the handwritten tagline "{argument name="tagline text" default="One script. One price."}" with an underline. In the bottom-right corner, add a small block of white sans-serif body copy, right-aligned, reading "{argument name="body copy" default="Analytics, heatmaps, A/B testing, and revenue attribution."}". Use an editorial ad-photography style with crisp text placement, clean hierarchy, high contrast, subtle paper realism, and a limited palette of black, white, and hot pink.

content: Create a minimalist static SaaS ad on a warm off-white background with elegant editorial typography and clean, high-fidelity text rendering. At the top, place a large black serif headline in two lines reading: "{argument name="headline text" default="Your dev team shouldn’t be your analytics team."}" The words “dev team” should appear in italic serif while the rest remains bold high-contrast serif, with generous spacing and a luxury magazine-style layout. Centered below, show a slightly tilted white paper invoice sheet with a soft drop shadow and a subtle vertical fold crease down the middle, as if photographed from above. The invoice uses black monospaced typewriter-style text and reads exactly: “INVOICE — DEV HOURS, OCTOBER” as the header, then 5 line items with hours and prices aligned in columns: “A/B test setup on pricing page   8 hrs   $1,200”, “GTM tag install (3 events)   4 hrs   $600”, “Funnel tracking debug   6 hrs   $900”, “Meta pixel mapping   3 hrs   $450”, and “Attribution reporting build   12 hrs   $1,800”. Under the 5 items, add a dashed divider line, then a bold monospaced “TOTAL” on the lower left and the total price “$4,950” on the lower right. Draw a hand-drawn yellow line striking through the total price. Add 4 doodle-style annotation elements around the invoice: on the left, handwritten yellow text saying “every month??” with a yellow arrow pointing right toward the paper; on the upper right of the paper, a yellow starburst scribble; below the total, a green curved arrow pointing up toward the crossed-out price; and on the lower right, a green hand-drawn circle containing the handwritten words “zero devs,” connected to the green arrow. At the bottom center of the ad, add a smaller black sans-serif body copy in 2 lines reading: “{argument name="body text" default="Marketers ship tests and track revenue themselves.\nNo tickets. No tag manager. One script."}" Keep the composition airy, premium, and conversion-focused, blending modern startup ad design with tactile paper realism and playful marker annotations.

content: Create a premium minimalist keynote-style presentation cover slide in an Apple-inspired industrial design aesthetic. Use a wide 16:9 layout with a deep matte black upper background and a pale concrete-gray lower platform occupying the bottom quarter of the frame. Place a large photorealistic floating rectangular slab in the right-center foreground, resembling a thin black glass device or polished obsidian platform with mirror-like reflections, chamfered chrome-metal edges, and precise industrial finishing. The slab should hover above the light surface and cast a soft elongated shadow directly beneath it. Add a tall vertical gray metallic wall or pillar on the far right edge to frame the composition. Use extremely sparse, high-end art direction, lots of negative space, cinematic studio lighting, subtle gradients, and crisp contrast between black and silver materials. On the left side, set clean white sans-serif typography with a clear hierarchy: a tiny top caption reading "{argument name="system label" default="Apple-inspired presentation system"}", a very large bold headline reading "{argument name="headline text" default="Clarity At Scale"}", and a medium subtitle reading "{argument name="subtitle text" default="The strongest slides behave like products."}". Below that, include exactly 3 numbered principles in a vertical list with thin separators and refined spacing: "01 | {argument name="principle 1" default="One focal object"}", "02 | Binary light / dark rhythm", and "03 | Blue only for signal". Add a short thin horizontal rule above the list and a tiny blue accent line below the list. Draw a very fine horizontal guide line near the top spanning most of the slide, with the small page number "01" at the upper right. Repeat the small caption "Apple-inspired presentation system" once again at the bottom left on the light surface. Overall look: luxury tech presentation, editorial product-launch slide, ultra-clean Swiss grid, restrained monochrome palette with only one subtle blue accent, photorealistic rendering blended with presentation design.

content: Create a clean modern Chinese tech blog cover in a wide 16:9 layout with a soft light-gray background, rounded-corner canvas, and minimalist dashboard aesthetic. On the left, place a small rounded category pill near the top with a yellow dot icon and the text “VPS 实测 · 购买参考”. Below it, set a very large bold dark navy Chinese headline in two stacked lines: “DMIT涨价之后” and “我把搬瓦工”. Under the headline, add a short yellow underline accent and then a medium-gray subheading reading “先看重点再下单”. The typography should feel premium, editorial, and highly legible, with strong contrast and generous spacing. On the right, show a floating desktop-app style server monitoring panel with a white card body, subtle shadow, rounded corners, and a pale beige browser top bar with three small circular window buttons. The panel header should read “BandwagonHost – CN2 GIA”, with a smaller line underneath “洛杉矶 · CN2 GIA” and a green status label “运行中”, plus on the top-right a small clock icon and the text “运行时长 27 天”. Inside the panel, include exactly 5 metric cards across the top row labeled “CPU” showing “12%”, “内存” showing “28%”, “硬盘” showing “18%”, “带宽” showing “1.2 Mbps”, and “延迟（本地）” showing “156 ms”, each with a small teal icon and a thin teal progress bar. content: Express {argument name="subject" default="theme/subject"} in a graffiti sketch style, presenting an overall visual effect of quick outlines, free deformation, improvised hand-drawing, and draft-like sketches. The lines are casual, exaggerated, varying in thickness, and slightly messy but rhythmic and expressive, emphasizing generalization, exaggeration, fun, and spontaneity rather than rigorous realism or fine detail. The colors are expressed in rough blocks with a distinct dry-brush feel, retaining uneven smears, brush marks, fly-white, and layering. Colors automatically adapt to the {argument name="subject" default="theme/subject"}, but the overall expression remains graffiti-like, sketch-like, and generalized. No transparent watercolor smudging effects, no delicate watercolor transitions, no paper textures, no soft atomization, and no dreamy textures. The background is mainly white space, maintaining a sense of simplicity, ease, unfinishedness, and design. Small amounts of auxiliary symbols, arrows, marks, circles, repeated lines, handwritten text, or other graffiti elements can be added to enhance the sketchbook or essay-like visual language, but they should not be too crowded or destroy the subject and the white space atmosphere. The content of the picture does not need to be written in advance; {argument name="subject" default="theme/subject"} will automatically deduce and generate the most suitable main image, actions, related elements, symbols, or simplified scenes. The overall style remains a unified graffiti sketch style and an exaggerated, generalized expression, avoiding complex realistic backgrounds and excessive detail. A special signature 'voxcat' should be naturally added as part of the picture, in a low-key but clear position such as the bottom left, bottom right, or near the title. The style should be unified with the overall layout, like an artist's signature or a design mark; the signature font should be exquisite, restrained, and high-end, not too large, and should not destroy the main composition or appear abrupt or cheap.

Each glass panel illustrates a different stage of transformation using geometric patterns in white and {argument name="accent color" default="blue"}. From left to right, the panels feature: floating dashes, a 3x3 grid of concentric circles, a dense dot matrix, scattered white pixel blocks, horizontal dashed lines, a fine mesh grid, and finally 3D cubic structures. The lighting is bright, soft, and high-key, creating a pristine corporate tech aesthetic with a shallow depth of field that gently blurs the furthest panels.
